cards.exampleEu: Eu moro em Florianópolis.
Ontem eu morei no centro.
Antes eu morava longe.
Eu já tinha morado ali.
Em julho eu morarei aqui.
Eu moraria perto da praia.
Espero que eu more bem.
Se eu morasse aqui, seria melhor.
Quando eu morar aqui, vou decorar tudo.
More perto do trabalho.
Estou morando com amigos.
Para morarmos juntos, precisamos planejar.
cards.infoNote: O infinitivo pessoal combina em frases tipo “para …”, “antes de …”.

cards.exampleEu: Eu atendo clientes online.

Para abrirmos a conta, faltam documentos.

Um dia eu serei professor.
Eu seria mais direto.
É importante que eu seja claro.
Se eu fosse você, aceitaria.
Quando eu for ao Rio, aviso.
Seja paciente.

cards.infoNote: Não se usa normalmente com *ser*: “estar sendo…” é marcado/regional em PT-BR.

cards.exampleEu: Para sermos melhores, precisamos praticar.
Eu estou em casa.

Eu tinha estado lá antes.
Amanhã eu estarei livre.

Para estarmos bem, precisamos descansar.
Eu tenho tempo hoje.

Eu tinha muitas dúvidas.

Amanhã eu terei resposta.
Eu teria mais calma.
Espero que eu tenha sorte.
Se eu tivesse tempo, iria.
Quando eu tiver dinheiro, viajo.
Tenha cuidado.

cards.infoNote: *Ter* quase não entra neste modelo com valor claro para estudo básico.

cards.exampleEu: Para termos sucesso, precisamos insistir.

Quando eu for ao banco, te aviso.
Vá com calma.

cards.infoNote: “Ir” + gerúndio existe, mas altera sentido (“ir fazendo…”); não é o foco básico do cartão.

cards.exampleEu: Para irmos juntos, precisamos combinar.

Espero que eu venha amanhã.
Se eu viesse antes, ajudaria.
Quando eu vier, te ligo.
Venha cá.

cards.infoNote: “Vir” + gerúndio não é foco aqui (“ir/vir fazendo…” é uso aparte).

cards.infoNote: O infinitivo pessoal aparece bem claro depois de *para*, *antes de*, *sem* … com sujeito tácito diferente.

cards.exampleEu: Eu vejo o painel porque alerta ficou alto.

cards.infoNote: Depois de *para*, *antes de* … aparece *vermos/verem* com sentido inequívoco de quem vê.

cards.infoNote: *Haver* quase não aparece em *estar + gerúndio* com sentido claro no básico; foque em construções como *há*, *havia*, *haja*.

Estou passeando entre builds pra olho focar longe.
Para passearmos juntos alinhamos tênis e horário de verão.
cards.infoNote: Primeira pessoa plural do subjuntivo *passeeemos* (com dois *e*) marca contraste com *passeamos* do presente.

morar-pi.note: Present indicative: current situation / where you habitually live.

morar-pp.note: Preterite: finished past event — here “morar no centro” implies staying overnight at a centre hotel/area.

morar-imp.note: Imperfect: descriptive / repeated situation in the past.

morar-mqp.note: Pluperfect compound (*tinha* + past participle): action completed before another past moment.

morar-cond.en: I would live near the beach.
morar-cond.note: Conditional (“would”), often hypothetical or softened statement.

morar-subj-pr.note: Present subjunctive after expression of emotion/wish (*espero que* …).

morar-subj-imp.en: If I lived here, it would be better.
morar-subj-imp.note: Imperfect subjunctive in unreal if-clauses + conditional consequence.

morar-subj-fut.en: When I live here, I'll decorate everything.
morar-subj-fut.note: Future subjunctive after *quando*: action not yet realised.

morar-ger.note: Progressive: *estar* + gerund — ongoing state right now.

morar-inf-p.en: For us to live together we need to plan.
morar-inf-p.note: Personal infinitive (*morarmos*) after *para* — clause with its own implicit subject (“we”).

atender-pi.en: I serve / attend to clients online.

atender-pp.note: Preterite: completed occurrences in customer service.

atender-imp.en: Before I used to work/serve at the counter.

atender-mqp.en: I had already assisted that customer.
atender-mqp.note: Pluperfect: anteriority to another narrative past.

atender-fut.en: Tomorrow I'll see clients early / start service early.

atender-cond.en: I'd gladly attend to you / serve you.
atender-cond.note: Conditional softens offer or hypothesis (*atender você* polite).

atender-subj-pr.en: It's important that I attend well.

atender-subj-imp.en: If I served (them) now, I'd fix it.

atender-subj-fut.en: When I finish serving, I'll tell you.

atender-inf-p.en: To serve them better we need time.

abrir-pi.en: I open the shop at nine.

abrir-pp.en: Yesterday I opened the window early.

abrir-imp.en: Before I opened the shop alone.

abrir-mqp.en: I had opened everything when he arrived.

abrir-cond.en: I'd open the door for you.

abrir-subj-pr.en: It's better that I open it now.

abrir-subj-imp.en: If I opened this, there would be a problem.

abrir-subj-fut.en: When I open the document, I'll send it to you.

abrir-inf-p.en: To open the account for us we're still missing paperwork.

ser-pp.en: Yesterday I was the first to arrive.

ser-imp.en: As a child I was shy.
ser-imp.note: Imperfect character description in the past.

ser-fut.en: One day I'll be a teacher.

ser-subj-pr.en: It's important that I be clear.
ser-subj-pr.note: *Que eu seja*: subjunctive after impersonal judgement.

ser-subj-imp.en: If I were you, I'd accept.
ser-subj-imp.note: Classic *se eu fosse você* idiom comparing perspective.

ser-subj-fut.en: When I go to Rio, I'll let you know.
ser-subj-fut.note: *Quando eu for* formal future subjunctive of *ser* / shared form with motion verbs in BR.

ser-ger.note: *Ser + gerund* (*estar sendo*) is seldom natural in Brazilian Portuguese; stylistically marked.

ser-inf-p.en: For us to improve we must practise.

estar-pp.en: Yesterday I was at the office.

estar-subj-pr.note: *Espero que + esteja* doubt about current state.

estar-subj-imp.en: If I were there, I'd help.

estar-inf-p.en: For us to be well we need to rest.

ter-mqp.en: I had never had that before.
ter-mqp.note: *Tinha tido* double auxiliary chain (very natural in BR).

ter-subj-imp.en: If I had time, I would go.

ter-subj-fut.en: When I have money, I travel.

ter-ger.note: *Ter* rarely forms a useful classroom example with *estar + gerúndio*; focus elsewhere.

ter-inf-p.en: For us to succeed we need to insist.

ir-pi.note: *Ir a* motion toward; also near-future nuance.

ir-imp.en: I used to walk to school.

ir-mqp.en: I had already gone there before.

ir-subj-pr.en: I hope it goes well for me.

ir-subj-imp.en: If I went today, I'd fix everything.

ir-subj-fut.en: When I go to the bank I let you know.

ir-ger.note: *Ir + gerúndio* (“ir fazendo…”) expresses gradual process — different lesson focus.

ir-inf-p.en: For us to go together we need to coordinate.

vir-imp.en: I used to come every morning.

vir-subj-imp.en: If I came earlier, I'd help.

vir-subj-fut.en: When I come I'll call you.

vir-ger.note: *Vir + gerúndio* builds a specialised progressive pattern (*vir fazendo…*).

vir-inf-p.en: For us to arrive early we need to leave now.

fazer-imp.en: Before I always used to do that.

fazer-mqp.en: I had already done the work.

fazer-subj-pr.en: It's better that I do it now.

fazer-subj-imp.en: If I did that, it would work out.

fazer-subj-fut.en: When I finish the report I'll send it.
fazer-subj-fut.note: *Quando fizer*: future subjunctive with projective plan.

fazer-inf-p.en: To do this well we're still short on time.

dizer-imp.en: Before I always used to say that.

dizer-fut.en: Tomorrow I'll give the answer / say the answer.

dizer-subj-pr.en: They want me to tell the truth.

dizer-subj-imp.en: If I said that, there'd be trouble.

dizer-subj-fut.en: When I say your name, come in.

dizer-inf-p.en: For us to say everything properly we need to think.
